sprint: Host-aware Bounds, actually enforced -- Scene/Place-hosted content self-heals to full bleed
task: A direct follow-up to the Hosted-by-Scene z-order fix: a user screenshot from World Builder showed a Scene-hosted Background Experience's Transform sliders reading X:1% Y:29% Width:99% Height:40% -- live, freely-draggable sliders, even though the Bounds panel right above them has always claimed 'Hosted by Scene -- this Experience fills the whole Scene (read-only)'. Root cause: _contentTransformFields() in tools/world-builder-v2/js/worldBuilderApp.js rendered fully editable X/Y/Width/Height sliders for every Experience regardless of Hosted By -- the Bounds panel's own claim was aspirational copy, never actually enforced, so a Scene-hosted (or Place-hosted) Experience could drift away from full-bleed exactly as the screenshot showed, which is what fed the earlier z-order bug in the first place. Fixed by gating _contentTransformFields() on the Experience's own hostedBy value: Scene/Place hosting now shows the locked note and no sliders at all, while also self-healing -- any already-drifted rect snaps back to full 0/0/1/1 the moment the Experience's Inspector is opened, since a Theme Author naturally reopens something to check on it rather than needing to manually reset four sliders by hand. Free hosting is completely unaffected -- still fully editable, as it should be. Verified via a real Playwright reproduction seeding the exact drifted values from the user's own screenshot, driving the real Workspace UI (open World -> Scenes -> open Scene -> click the Background Experience) with zero manual slider interaction, and confirming both the in-memory rect snapped to full bleed and a subsequent real Build produced target:'slide' with a correct full-bleed rect. Full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
